rag.description: Create a Google Tasks task in a list you provide (title or id) during an automation run, using either a direct OAuth token or a stored credentials reference. It enforces required inputs, supports optional fields like notes and due date, and saves the returned task identifiers and links as Base64 session parameters so later steps stay consistent and reliable.

qa.question: What does the NewGmailTask plugin do?
qa.answer: It creates a new Google Tasks task in a target list and stores the created task fields in namespaced session parameters for later workflow steps.

qa.question: What are the key features and parameters of NewGmailTask?
qa.answer: TaskList is required, while Title, Notes, and Due are optional. For authentication, provide either Token or Credentials, and the plugin writes key outputs to NewGmailTask:* session parameters.

qa.question: How does NewGmailTask fit into the integration context of an automation workflow?
qa.answer: It runs as an Action in a sequential workflow step (componentType “task”) using the google icon provider and the ActionRuleModel.

qa.question: What are best practices for using NewGmailTask in automations?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials for long-running runs, pass a list id when possible to avoid title ambiguity, use ISO 8601 UTC for Due (Z-suffix), and read outputs from NewGmailTask:* session parameters instead of re-calling the API.

qa.question: What rule or schema details should I follow when configuring NewGmailTask?
qa.answer: Use $type “Action” with pluginName “NewGmailTask” and pass arguments for TaskList plus optional Title, Notes, Due, and either Token or Credentials, then consume outputs from NewGmailTask:Id, Title, Status, SelfLink, and Position.

qa.question: What is the manifest key of this plugin?
What manifestVersion is used by this plugin?
What is the pluginType of NewGmailTask?
Which platforms are supported by this plugin?
Which categories is this plugin associated with?
qa.answer: The plugin is listed under the “GoogleTasks” category.

qa.question: Who is the author of this plugin manifest?
What author link is provided in the manifest metadata?
What is the summary of NewGmailTask?
qa.answer: It creates a new task in a Google Tasks list, authenticates with Token or Credentials, and stores key task fields in namespaced session parameters so later steps can reuse them safely.

qa.question: What source descriptions and Git links are relevant for NewGmailTask?
qa.answer: The manifest description and examples explain task creation, list resolution, optional fields, and Base64 session outputs under NewGmailTask:*. Documentation: https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/docs/Actions/NewGmailTask.md. Source: https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/src/G4.Plugins.Google/Actions/NewGmailTask.cs.

qa.question: How does NewGmailTask handle errors and edge cases?
qa.answer: It accounts for missing or invalid authentication, missing TaskList, ambiguous or missing list matches, invalid Due parsing, Google API quota or non-success responses, and missing required response fields like id, selfLink, position, or status.

description: ### Purpose
The NewGmailTask plugin creates a new Google Tasks task in a chosen task list during an automation run.
It helps workflows add tasks without opening Google Tasks manually, so bots can capture work items as they happen.
It supports authentication using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
It saves the created task details into session parameters so later steps can reuse the results reliably.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                    | Description                                                                                           |
| Create task                | Creates a new task in a target Google Tasks list using the Google Tasks API (Tasks: insert).          |
| List resolution            | Resolves the target list by id or by matching the provided list title.                                |
| Optional fields            | Supports optional task fields such as notes and due date/time when provided.                          |
| Token authentication       | Accepts a raw OAuth Bearer access token for direct API calls.                                         |
| Credentials authentication | Accepts a credentials id/name and exchanges it for a fresh access token at runtime.                   |
| Result session storage     | Stores created task fields in namespaced session parameters (NewGmailTask:) as Base64-encoded values. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                                | Description                                                                           |
| Convert incoming messages into tasks    | Create a follow-up task from an email, form submission, or chat message.              |
| Capture leads as tasks                  | Add a new task when a lead is detected in a CRM export or a website scrape result.    |
| Create recurring operational work items | Generate daily or weekly tasks based on schedules and checklist templates.            |
| Post-process job outcomes               | Create a task when a job fails or needs human review, including notes and a due date. |

edge_cases: Access token missing required scope for Google Tasks API
Google Tasks API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Multiple task lists match the TaskList title
No matching task list found for TaskList title/id
Response JSON missing required properties: id/selfLink/position/status
annotations.expected_result: A new task is created in the resolved list and task fields are stored in `NewGmailTask:*` session parameters (Base64).
annotations.notes: Creates a new task in a target list using a provided access token.

description: ### NewGmailTask: Create task using access token
This example creates a new task in a specified task list, with notes and a due date.

edge_cases: Credentials value is empty or whitespace-only
Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
Google Tasks API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
No matching task list found for TaskList id
Response JSON missing required properties: id/selfLink/position/status
annotations.expected_result: A new task is created in the resolved list and task fields are stored in `NewGmailTask:*` session parameters (Base64).
annotations.notes: Creates a new task by exchanging a stored credential record for a fresh access token.

description: ### NewGmailTask: Create task using credentials reference
This example creates a new task using a stored credentials record and a target list id.
Stores the unique task identifier returned by the Google Tasks API after a task is created.
Helps later workflow steps target the exact task for updates, lookups, or deletion.
Reduces mistakes by keeping a stable reference to the created task.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTask:Id

description: Stores the position value returned by the Google Tasks API for the created task.
Preserves ordering metadata that Google uses to arrange tasks inside the list.
Helps workflows that need to keep or validate task order across runs.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTask:Position

description: Stores the self link URL returned by the Google Tasks API for the created task.
Provides a direct resource reference that can help with troubleshooting and auditing.
Helps confirm the automation is pointing to the intended task resource.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTask:SelfLink

description: Stores the status returned by the Google Tasks API for the created task.
Confirms the initial state of the task, such as whether it still needs action.
Enables later steps to make decisions based on the stored state without an extra fetch.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTask:Status

description: Stores the final task title that was used when the task was created.
Keeps a reliable record when the title comes from defaults or expressions.
Supports consistent logging, display, and verification in later workflow steps.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTask:Title

description: Identifies a stored credential record by id or name so a fresh access token can be obtained at runtime.
Avoids hardcoding tokens by exchanging the stored refresh token for a new access token when needed.
Improves reliability for long-running automations where short-lived access tokens may expire.
parameters.name: Credentials

description: Provides an optional due date and time for the task being created.
Helps schedule the task and enables reminders or sorting in Google Tasks clients.
Converts the provided value to an ISO 8601 UTC format before sending it to the Google Tasks API.
parameters.name: Due

description: Provides optional notes that are saved as the task description in Google Tasks.
Adds extra context such as details, links, or instructions that help complete the task.
Improves task clarity without changing the task title or destination list.
parameters.name: Notes

description: Specifies which Google Tasks list will receive the new task by providing a list id or a list title.
Determines the exact destination for the task so it is created in the intended list.
Supports reliable targeting by resolving the list id from the provided value before creation.
parameters.name: TaskList

description: Sets the title for the task that will be created in the target Google Tasks list.
Controls how the task appears to users and helps keep tasks easy to find and understand.
Uses a timestamped default title when no value is provided to avoid creating unnamed tasks.
parameters.name: Title

description: Supplies an OAuth Bearer access token used to call the Google Tasks API.
Enables direct authentication for the request when no stored credential record is used.
Is ignored when a credentials reference is provided so stored credentials can take priority.
parameters.name: Token

summary: The NewGmailTask plugin creates a new task in a Google Tasks list during an automation run.
It can authenticate using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token.
It stores key task fields (like id, title, status, and self link) in namespaced session parameters so later workflow steps can reuse them safely.
